accurate.why: Tracks the one-state-license rule, in-state preference, reporting, and public-availability provisions.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; Interior/Secretary context is correct.

states_purpose.why: States the bill eases licensing for Interior real-property appraisals.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes it overrides prior state-specific licensure requirements for these transactions.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes out-of-state appraiser may be used only if in-state is unavailable/unqualified/not cost competitive.

effective_dates.why: Notes the public-availability requirement; omits exact deadlines but captures key duties.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures licensing change, preference, and transparency; omits reporting requirement but core effect intact.

correct_entities.why: Department/Secretary of the Interior named correctly.

judged_via: claude-opus-4-8 via parallel Claude Code subagents (full bill text, web/knowledge-verified)
